Repurp: Drug–Gene Interaction Network Visualisation

A Complete Workflow for Drug Repurposing Studies

Lucas VH Tran

2026-06-09

Introduction

Repurp provides a unified, multi-backend workflow for building and visualising drug–gene / biomarker interaction networks, with a focus on atopic dermatitis (AD) drug repurposing. It ships seven complementary visualisation backends, a curated AD drug reference table, and a built-in colour system shared across all plot types.

Visualisation backends:

# Function(s) Engine Output Best for
1 plot_dgi_igraph() / plot_dgi_igraph_core() igraph + qgraph SVG + PNG Fast bipartite, large networks
2 plot_dgi_tripartite_igraph() igraph + qgraph SVG + PNG Gene × Drug × Pathway (base R)
3 plot_dgi_ggraph() / plot_dgi_ggraph_core() ggraph + tidygraph SVG + PNG Tripartite, publication panels
4 plot_dgi_ggraph_enhanced() ggraph + ggrepel + ggnewscale SVG + PNG Hub-labelled FR-layout, journals
5 plot_dgi_ad_network() ggplot2 + ggrepel SVG + PNG Curated AD drug–biomarker network
6 build_visnetwork() / build_visnetwork_tripartite() visNetwork Interactive HTML Exploratory, supplementary data
7 plot_chord_dgi() / plot_chord_dgi_combined() circlize SVG Drug class overview, talks
8 plot_dgi_heatmap() ggplot2 SVG + PNG Drug × Gene tile matrix
9 plot_dgi_sankey() ggplot2 + ggalluvial SVG + PNG Drug class → Drug → Gene flow
10 plot_dgi_dotmatrix() ggplot2 SVG + PNG Gene × Drug-class bubble matrix

Installation

# Install from local source build
install.packages("path/to/Repurp", repos = NULL, type = "source")

# Core suggested packages (all visualisations)
install.packages(c(
  "ggplot2", "ggrepel", "ggnewscale",   # ggraph-family plots
  "ggraph", "tidygraph",                 # ggraph layouts
  "visNetwork",                          # interactive HTML
  "circlize",                            # chord diagrams
  "ggalluvial"                           # Sankey flow diagram
))

Package data

library(Repurp)
library(dplyr)

Curated AD drugs

repurp_ad_drugs() returns a tibble of 18 established AD treatments with drug class and molecular targets:

repurp_ad_drugs()
#> # A tibble: 18 × 4
#>    drug_name      brand      category              targets_known      
#>    <chr>          <chr>      <chr>                 <chr>              
#>  1 Hydrocortisone ""         Corticosteroid        NR3C1 (GR)         
#>  2 Triamcinolone  ""         Corticosteroid        NR3C1 (GR)         
#>  3 Betamethasone  ""         Corticosteroid        NR3C1 (GR)         
#>  4 Tacrolimus     "Protopic" Calcineurin inhibitor FKBP1A; PPP3CA     
#>  5 Pimecrolimus   "Elidel"   Calcineurin inhibitor FKBP1A; PPP3CA     
#>  6 Cyclosporine   ""         Calcineurin inhibitor PPP3CA; PPP3CB     
#>  7 Crisaborole    "Eucrisa"  PDE4 inhibitor        PDE4B; PDE4D       
#>  8 Roflumilast    "Zoryve"   PDE4 inhibitor        PDE4A; PDE4B; PDE4D
#>  9 Ruxolitinib    "Opzelura" JAK inhibitor         JAK1; JAK2         
#> 10 Upadacitinib   "Rinvoq"   JAK inhibitor         JAK1               
#> 11 Abrocitinib    "Cibinqo"  JAK inhibitor         JAK1               
#> 12 Baricitinib    "Olumiant" JAK inhibitor         JAK1; JAK2         
#> 13 Dupilumab      "Dupixent" Biologic              IL4R               
#> 14 Tralokinumab   "Adbry"    Biologic              IL13               
#> 15 Lebrikizumab   "Ebglyss"  Biologic              IL13               
#> 16 Nemolizumab    "Nemluvio" Biologic              IL31RA             
#> 17 Tapinarof      "Vtama"    AhR agonist           AHR                
#> 18 Methotrexate   ""         Immunosuppressant     DHFR; ATIC

Curated MOA edges

repurp_ad_edges() returns 106 mechanism-of-action (MOA) edges linking each AD drug to biomarker genes with edge type ("direct" or "indirect"):

repurp_ad_edges() |> dplyr::count(from, edge_type) |> head(12)
#> # A tibble: 12 × 3
#>    from           edge_type     n
#>    <chr>          <chr>     <int>
#>  1 Abrocitinib    indirect      7
#>  2 Baricitinib    indirect      7
#>  3 Betamethasone  indirect      8
#>  4 Crisaborole    indirect      5
#>  5 Cyclosporine   indirect      7
#>  6 Dupilumab      direct        1
#>  7 Hydrocortisone indirect     11
#>  8 Lebrikizumab   direct        1
#>  9 Methotrexate   indirect     10
#> 10 Nemolizumab    indirect      3
#> 11 Pimecrolimus   indirect      5
#> 12 Roflumilast    indirect      7

Colour palettes

# Edge / interaction-type palette (14 types)
data.frame(type = names(dgi_edge_colors), hex = dgi_edge_colors)
#>                            type     hex
#> INHIBITOR             INHIBITOR #E41A1C
#> AGONIST                 AGONIST #377EB8
#> ANTAGONIST           ANTAGONIST #FF7F00
#> ACTIVATOR             ACTIVATOR #4DAF4A
#> BINDER                   BINDER #984EA3
#> BLOCKER                 BLOCKER #A65628
#> MODULATOR             MODULATOR #F781BF
#> SUBSTRATE             SUBSTRATE #66C2A5
#> Inverse Agonist Inverse Agonist #FB8072
#> Antibody               Antibody #8DD3C7
#> Cleavage               Cleavage #BEBADA
#> Other                     Other #BDBDBD
#> None                       None #999999
#> Curated MOA         Curated MOA #404040

# Node-type palette
dgi_node_colors
#>      Gene      Drug   Pathway 
#> "#009E73" "#F0E442" "#6A3D9A"

# Drug-class palette (13 classes)
data.frame(class = names(repurp_class_colors()), hex = repurp_class_colors())
#>                                       class     hex
#> Corticosteroid               Corticosteroid #E41A1C
#> Calcineurin inhibitor Calcineurin inhibitor #FF7F00
#> PDE4 inhibitor               PDE4 inhibitor #377EB8
#> JAK inhibitor                 JAK inhibitor #4DAF4A
#> Biologic                           Biologic #984EA3
#> AhR agonist                     AhR agonist #F781BF
#> Immunosuppressant         Immunosuppressant #A65628
#> Inhibitor                         Inhibitor #FB9A99
#> Agonist                             Agonist #A6CEE3
#> Antagonist                       Antagonist #FDBF6F
#> Binder                               Binder #CAB2D6
#> Activator                         Activator #B2DF8A
#> Other                                 Other #BDBDBD

Synthetic demo dataset

All examples below use a small in-memory dataset built from the curated AD data, so you can run the vignette without a DGIdb download.

# Compact synthetic DGI table (30 gene–drug edges)
synth_dgi <- tibble::tribble(
  ~Gene,     ~Drug,             ~InteractionType,
  "IL13",    "Dupilumab",       "INHIBITOR",
  "IL4R",    "Dupilumab",       "INHIBITOR",
  "IL13",    "Tralokinumab",    "INHIBITOR",
  "IL13",    "Lebrikizumab",    "INHIBITOR",
  "JAK1",    "Upadacitinib",    "INHIBITOR",
  "JAK1",    "Abrocitinib",     "INHIBITOR",
  "JAK1",    "Baricitinib",     "INHIBITOR",
  "JAK2",    "Baricitinib",     "INHIBITOR",
  "JAK2",    "Ruxolitinib",     "INHIBITOR",
  "JAK1",    "Ruxolitinib",     "INHIBITOR",
  "PDE4B",   "Crisaborole",     "INHIBITOR",
  "PDE4D",   "Crisaborole",     "INHIBITOR",
  "PDE4A",   "Roflumilast",     "INHIBITOR",
  "PDE4B",   "Roflumilast",     "INHIBITOR",
  "IL31RA",  "Nemolizumab",     "INHIBITOR",
  "AHR",     "Tapinarof",       "AGONIST",
  "NR3C1",   "Hydrocortisone",  "AGONIST",
  "NR3C1",   "Triamcinolone",   "AGONIST",
  "NR3C1",   "Betamethasone",   "AGONIST",
  "FKBP1A",  "Tacrolimus",      "BINDER",
  "FKBP1A",  "Pimecrolimus",    "BINDER",
  "PPP3CA",  "Tacrolimus",      "INHIBITOR",
  "PPP3CA",  "Cyclosporine",    "INHIBITOR",
  "DHFR",    "Methotrexate",    "INHIBITOR",
  "ATIC",    "Methotrexate",    "INHIBITOR",
  "IL18",    "Hydrocortisone",  "MODULATOR",
  "IL22",    "Triamcinolone",   "MODULATOR",
  "IL33",    "Betamethasone",   "MODULATOR",
  "STAT6",   "Upadacitinib",    "INHIBITOR",
  "TSLP",    "Dupilumab",       "MODULATOR"
)

# Verify the standardised structure
glimpse(synth_dgi)
#> Rows: 30
#> Columns: 3
#> $ Gene            <chr> "IL13", "IL4R", "IL13", "IL13", "JAK1", "JAK1", "JAK1"…
#> $ Drug            <chr> "Dupilumab", "Dupilumab", "Tralokinumab", "Lebrikizuma…
#> $ InteractionType <chr> "INHIBITOR", "INHIBITOR", "INHIBITOR", "INHIBITOR", "I…

Preparing DGIdb data (your own files)

If you have downloaded interaction data from DGIdb v5, use prepare_dgi_data() to standardise it:

net_dat <- prepare_dgi_data(
  interactions = "interactions.tsv",   # path to DGIdb TSV download
  gene_list    = c("IL13", "IL4R", "JAK1", "JAK2", "PDE4B", "TSLP")
)

prepare_dgi_data() normalises column names, coerces NA/empty interaction types to "None", and filters to your gene list.

Part 1: Candidate prioritisation and coverage analysis

The analysis helpers convert visual network edges into ranked repurposing tables. They accept the package’s common edge formats, including Gene / Drug / InteractionType, DGIdb-style columns, and from / to edge lists.

dgi_std <- standardize_dgi_edges(synth_dgi)
dgi_std |> head(8)
#> # A tibble: 8 × 3
#>   gene  drug         interaction_type
#>   <chr> <chr>        <chr>           
#> 1 IL13  Dupilumab    INHIBITOR       
#> 2 IL4R  Dupilumab    INHIBITOR       
#> 3 IL13  Tralokinumab INHIBITOR       
#> 4 IL13  Lebrikizumab INHIBITOR       
#> 5 JAK1  Upadacitinib INHIBITOR       
#> 6 JAK1  Abrocitinib  INHIBITOR       
#> 7 JAK1  Baricitinib  INHIBITOR       
#> 8 JAK2  Baricitinib  INHIBITOR

1a - Rank candidate drugs

score_drug_repurposing() ranks drugs by weighted biomarker coverage. Use gene_weights to prioritise biomarkers from differential-expression, proteomics, GWAS, or pathway analyses.

biomarkers <- c("IL13", "IL4R", "JAK1", "JAK2", "PDE4B", "TSLP")

ranked_drugs <- score_drug_repurposing(
  dgi_std,
  biomarkers = biomarkers,
  gene_weights = c(IL13 = 2, IL4R = 2, JAK1 = 1.5, JAK2 = 1, PDE4B = 1, TSLP = 1.5)
)

ranked_drugs |>
  dplyr::select(rank, drug, category, score, target_count, coverage, targets)
#> # A tibble: 9 × 7
#>    rank drug         category       score target_count coverage targets         
#>   <int> <chr>        <chr>          <dbl>        <int>    <dbl> <chr>           
#> 1     1 Dupilumab    Biologic         5.5            3    0.5   IL13; IL4R; TSLP
#> 2     2 Baricitinib  JAK inhibitor    2.5            2    0.333 JAK1; JAK2      
#> 3     3 Ruxolitinib  JAK inhibitor    2.5            2    0.333 JAK1; JAK2      
#> 4     4 Lebrikizumab Biologic         2              1    0.167 IL13            
#> 5     5 Tralokinumab Biologic         2              1    0.167 IL13            
#> 6     6 Abrocitinib  JAK inhibitor    1.5            1    0.167 JAK1            
#> 7     7 Upadacitinib JAK inhibitor    1.5            1    0.167 JAK1            
#> 8     8 Crisaborole  PDE4 inhibitor   1              1    0.167 PDE4B           
#> 9     9 Roflumilast  PDE4 inhibitor   1              1    0.167 PDE4B

1b - Biomarker and class coverage

Use summarize_biomarker_coverage() to find well-covered biomarkers and targets that may need additional evidence sources. Use summarize_drug_classes() to summarise the therapeutic classes represented in a network.

summarize_biomarker_coverage(dgi_std, biomarkers = biomarkers) |>
  dplyr::select(gene, drug_count, drug_class_count, drugs)
#> # A tibble: 6 × 4
#>   gene  drug_count drug_class_count drugs                                       
#>   <chr>      <int>            <int> <chr>                                       
#> 1 JAK1           4                1 Abrocitinib; Baricitinib; Ruxolitinib; Upad…
#> 2 IL13           3                1 Dupilumab; Lebrikizumab; Tralokinumab       
#> 3 JAK2           2                1 Baricitinib; Ruxolitinib                    
#> 4 PDE4B          2                1 Crisaborole; Roflumilast                    
#> 5 IL4R           1                1 Dupilumab                                   
#> 6 TSLP           1                1 Dupilumab

summarize_drug_classes(dgi_std, biomarkers = biomarkers) |>
  dplyr::select(category, drug_count, target_count, coverage, drugs)
#> # A tibble: 3 × 5
#>   category       drug_count target_count coverage drugs                         
#>   <chr>               <int>        <int>    <dbl> <chr>                         
#> 1 Biologic                3            3    0.5   Dupilumab; Lebrikizumab; Tral…
#> 2 JAK inhibitor           4            2    0.333 Abrocitinib; Baricitinib; Rux…
#> 3 PDE4 inhibitor          2            1    0.167 Crisaborole; Roflumilast

1c - Expression reversal scoring

score_expression_reversal() combines DGI edges with differential-expression statistics. Positive scores indicate interactions expected to oppose the disease expression direction, for example inhibition of an up-regulated biomarker.

deg_stats <- tibble::tibble(
  gene = c("IL13", "IL4R", "JAK1", "JAK2", "PDE4B", "TSLP"),
  logFC = c(1.4, 1.1, 0.7, 0.5, 0.9, 1.2),
  padj = c(0.001, 0.002, 0.02, 0.04, 0.01, 0.005)
)

score_expression_reversal(dgi_std, deg_stats) |>
  dplyr::select(rank, drug, category, reversal_score, supportive_edges,
                conflicting_edges, targets)
#> # A tibble: 9 × 7
#>    rank drug  category reversal_score supportive_edges conflicting_edges targets
#>   <int> <chr> <chr>             <dbl>            <int>             <int> <chr>  
#> 1     1 Dupi… Biologic           7.17                2                 0 IL13; …
#> 2     2 Lebr… Biologic           4.2                 1                 0 IL13   
#> 3     3 Tral… Biologic           4.2                 1                 0 IL13   
#> 4     4 Bari… JAK inh…           1.89                2                 0 JAK1; …
#> 5     5 Ruxo… JAK inh…           1.89                2                 0 JAK1; …
#> 6     6 Cris… PDE4 in…           1.8                 1                 0 PDE4B  
#> 7     7 Rofl… PDE4 in…           1.8                 1                 0 PDE4B  
#> 8     8 Abro… JAK inh…           1.19                1                 0 JAK1   
#> 9     9 Upad… JAK inh…           1.19                1                 0 JAK1

For curated AD mechanism-of-action edges, direction is detected automatically:

score_drug_repurposing(
  repurp_ad_edges(),
  biomarkers = biomarkers
) |>
  dplyr::select(rank, drug, category, score, target_count, coverage, targets) |>
  head(10)
#> # A tibble: 10 × 7
#>     rank drug           category             score target_count coverage targets
#>    <int> <chr>          <chr>                <dbl>        <int>    <dbl> <chr>  
#>  1     1 Abrocitinib    JAK inhibitor            2            2    0.333 IL13; …
#>  2     2 Baricitinib    JAK inhibitor            2            2    0.333 IL13; …
#>  3     3 Betamethasone  Corticosteroid           2            2    0.333 IL13; …
#>  4     4 Crisaborole    PDE4 inhibitor           2            2    0.333 IL13; …
#>  5     5 Cyclosporine   Calcineurin inhibit…     2            2    0.333 IL13; …
#>  6     6 Hydrocortisone Corticosteroid           2            2    0.333 IL13; …
#>  7     7 Methotrexate   Immunosuppressant        2            2    0.333 IL13; …
#>  8     8 Nemolizumab    Biologic                 2            2    0.333 IL13; …
#>  9     9 Pimecrolimus   Calcineurin inhibit…     2            2    0.333 IL13; …
#> 10    10 Roflumilast    PDE4 inhibitor           2            2    0.333 IL13; …

Part 2: igraph / qgraph static networks

The igraph backend draws using base-R plot.igraph() with a Fruchterman-Reingold layout computed by qgraph. Output is written to SVG + PNG when out_dir is specified.

Layout algorithm

The layout is computed by minimising the energy function:

\[ E(X) = \sum_{u < v} \left[ k^2 \frac{1}{\|x_u - x_v\|} \;-\; \|x_u - x_v\|^2 \mathbf{1}_{\{u,v\}\in E} \right] \]

where \(k = \sqrt{A / n}\) is the optimal pairwise distance (\(A\) = canvas area, \(n\) = node count). Nodes repel uniformly but only connected pairs attract.

1a — Full bipartite network

plot_dgi_igraph(
  net_dat     = synth_dgi,
  title       = "AD Drug–Biomarker Network",
  gene_color  = "#009E73",
  drug_color  = "#F0E442",
  vertex_size = 7,
  label_cex   = 0.75
  # out_dir = "results/"  # uncomment to save SVG + PNG
)

Key parameters:

Parameter Default Effect
vertex_size 6 Node circle radius
label_cex 0.7 Label scaling factor
area_factor 2.0 Canvas area multiplier — increase to spread nodes
repulse_factor 1.0 Node repulsion strength
gene_color "#009E73" Gene node fill
drug_color "#F0E442" Drug node fill

1b — Core network (hub genes only)

plot_dgi_igraph_core() filters to genes with ≥ min_gene_edges drug connections before plotting:

plot_dgi_igraph_core(
  net_dat        = synth_dgi,
  title          = "Core AD Network — genes ≥ 2 drugs",
  min_gene_edges = 2L
)

1c — Tripartite: Gene × Drug × Pathway

plot_dgi_tripartite_igraph() accepts a third node type — Pathway — via pathway_edges. Without pathway edges it reduces to the bipartite case.

# Build pathway_edges from STRING enrichment (see rbioapi)
library(rbioapi)
enrich <- rbioapi::rba_string_enrichment(ids = unique(synth_dgi$Gene), species = 9606)
pathway_edges <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  enrich[["KEGG"]] |> dplyr::mutate(category = "KEGG"),
  enrich[["Process"]] |> dplyr::mutate(category = "GO_Process")
) |>
  dplyr::filter(fdr < 0.05) |>
  tidyr::unnest(inputGenes) |>
  dplyr::select(from = inputGenes, to = description, edge_type = category)

dgi_e <- dplyr::select(synth_dgi, from = Gene, to = Drug, edge_type = InteractionType)

plot_dgi_tripartite_igraph(
  dgi_edges     = dgi_e,
  pathway_edges = pathway_edges,
  title         = "Tripartite: Gene × Drug × Pathway",
  out_dir       = "results/"
)

Part 3: ggraph publication-quality networks

The ggraph backend returns a ggplot object — fully composable with patchwork, cowplot, and standard ggplot2 theming.

# Convert to the from/to/edge_type format expected by all ggraph functions
dgi_e <- dplyr::transmute(
  synth_dgi,
  from      = Gene,
  to        = Drug,
  edge_type = InteractionType
)

2a — Full tripartite view

p_full <- plot_dgi_ggraph(
  dgi_edges        = dgi_e,
  title            = "Drug–Gene Network (ggraph FR layout)",
  min_drug_targets = 1L,
  seed             = 42L
)

p_full

2b — Core view (Kamada-Kawai layout)

plot_dgi_ggraph_core() uses a Kamada-Kawai layout (default) and labels every retained node:

p_core <- plot_dgi_ggraph_core(
  dgi_edges      = dgi_e,
  title          = "Core Drug–Gene Network (KK layout)",
  min_gene_edges = 2L,
  seed           = 42L
)

p_core

2c — Enhanced: FR layout + ggrepel + ggnewscale

plot_dgi_ggraph_enhanced() uses a qgraph FR layout with separate colour scales for nodes and edges (via ggnewscale), and ggrepel for non-overlapping labels. Two modes are available:

Full mode

p_enh_full <- plot_dgi_ggraph_enhanced(
  dgi_edges        = dgi_e,
  mode             = "full",
  title            = "Enhanced DGI Network — full view",
  min_drug_targets = 2L,
  seed             = 42L
)

p_enh_full

Core mode

p_enh_core <- plot_dgi_ggraph_enhanced(
  dgi_edges      = dgi_e,
  mode           = "core",
  title          = "Enhanced DGI Network — core view",
  min_gene_edges = 2L,
  seed           = 42L
)

p_enh_core

Mode comparison

Parameter "full" "core"
Gene filter none genes ≥ min_gene_edges drugs
Drug labels hub drugs only (≥ min_drug_targets) all drugs
Layout area 2 × n²·⁰⁵ same
ggrepel force 0.5 1.0
Use case complete landscape focused hub view

2d — AD drug–biomarker network

plot_dgi_ad_network() draws the curated AD drugs with drug-class colour coding and MOA edges:

# Filter curated edges to genes present in our demo dataset
ad_e <- repurp_ad_edges() |>
  dplyr::filter(to %in% unique(synth_dgi$Gene))

p_adn <- plot_dgi_ad_network(
  edges     = ad_e,
  drug_info = repurp_ad_drugs(),
  title     = "AD Treatment Drug–Biomarker Network"
)

p_adn

Drug nodes are coloured by therapeutic category. Solid edges = direct molecular target or DGIdb confirmed; dashed edges = pathway-level regulation.

Part 4: Interactive visNetwork HTML widget

build_visnetwork() returns a visNetwork htmlwidget for in-document rendering (RMarkdown / Quarto) or standalone HTML export.

3a — Bipartite widget

vn_bip <- build_visnetwork(
  net_dat = synth_dgi,
  title   = "Interactive AD Drug–Biomarker Network"
)
vn_bip

3b — Tripartite widget

# Requires pathway_edges data frame (see Part 1c for how to build it)
vn_tri <- build_visnetwork_tripartite(
  net_dat       = synth_dgi,
  pathway_edges = pathway_edges,
  title         = "Tripartite Interactive Network"
)
vn_tri

3c — Save to HTML

save_visnetwork(vn_bip, "results/Fig_DGI_interactive.html", selfcontained = TRUE)

selfcontained = TRUE embeds all JavaScript and CSS in a single portable .html file (recommended for supplementary data submissions).

Part 5: Chord diagrams (circlize)

Chord diagrams show drug-class sectors on one arc and gene sectors on the other, with link ribbons coloured by interaction type. The adjacency matrix used for plotting is returned invisibly.

4a — Basic chord diagram

# Build chord-ready edges from curated AD data
chord_e <- repurp_ad_edges() |>
  dplyr::filter(to %in% unique(synth_dgi$Gene)) |>
  dplyr::left_join(
    repurp_ad_drugs() |> dplyr::select(drug_name, class = category),
    by = c("from" = "drug_name")
  ) |>
  dplyr::rename(drug = from, gene = to) |>
  dplyr::mutate(int_type = "Curated MOA")

plot_chord_dgi(
  edges = chord_e,
  title = "AD Drugs × Biomarker Chord (curated)"
)

Key parameters:

Parameter Default Effect
transparency 0.15 Link ribbon opacity (0 = opaque)
min_drug_targets 1L Drop drugs with fewer gene targets
gene_color "#009E73" Gene sector colour
svg_w, svg_h 32, 32 SVG output dimensions (inches)

4b — Combined chord: curated AD + DGIdb hub drugs

plot_chord_dgi_combined() merges curated MOA edges with DGIdb hub drugs (≥ min_dgidb_targets genes):

# Build DGIdb hub edges (requires your own DGIdb data)
dgi_chord <- dplyr::transmute(synth_dgi,
  drug = Drug, gene = Gene, int_type = InteractionType
)

curated_chord <- repurp_ad_edges() |>
  dplyr::filter(to %in% unique(synth_dgi$Gene)) |>
  dplyr::left_join(
    repurp_ad_drugs() |> dplyr::select(drug_name, class = category),
    by = c("from" = "drug_name")
  ) |>
  dplyr::rename(drug = from, gene = to) |>
  dplyr::mutate(int_type = "Curated MOA")

plot_chord_dgi_combined(
  dgi_edges     = dgi_chord,
  curated_edges = curated_chord,
  title         = "AD + DGIdb Hub Drugs — Combined Chord",
  min_dgidb_targets = 2L,
  out_dir       = "results/"
)

Part 6: Tile heatmap — Drug × Gene matrix

plot_dgi_heatmap() renders a ggplot2 tile matrix where rows are drugs sorted by drug class then degree, columns are genes sorted by degree, and fill encodes the dominant interaction type for each drug–gene pair.

This plot is ideal for publications where a compact, annotation-rich overview of the entire drug × gene space is needed.

p_heat <- plot_dgi_heatmap(
  dgi_edges  = dgi_e,
  drug_info  = repurp_ad_drugs(),
  title      = "Drug × Gene Interaction Heatmap",
  max_drugs  = 20L,
  max_genes  = 15L
)

p_heat

Key parameters:

Parameter Default Effect
max_drugs 60L Top-N drugs by degree to display
max_genes 50L Top-N genes by degree to display
drug_info NULL Data frame with drug_name + category for row strips
edge_palette dgi_edge_colors Named hex vector for interaction types

Reading the heatmap: Each cell represents a drug–gene pair with at least one recorded interaction. The fill colour shows the most frequent interaction type between that drug and gene. White cells indicate absence of interaction. Rows are grouped by drug class, making it easy to spot within-class gene targeting patterns.

Part 7: Sankey / alluvial flow diagram

plot_dgi_sankey() renders a three-axis alluvial plot (requires ggalluvial):

\[ \text{Drug Class} \;\longrightarrow\; \text{Drug} \;\longrightarrow\; \text{Gene} \]

Band width is proportional to the number of connections. Colour can encode either drug class (fill_by = "drug_class") or interaction type (fill_by = "edge_type").

6a — Colour by drug class

p_sank_cls <- plot_dgi_sankey(
  dgi_edges  = dgi_e,
  drug_info  = repurp_ad_drugs(),
  fill_by    = "drug_class",
  title      = "Drug Class → Drug → Gene Flow (by class)",
  max_genes  = 15L
)

p_sank_cls

6b — Colour by interaction type

p_sank_typ <- plot_dgi_sankey(
  dgi_edges  = dgi_e,
  drug_info  = repurp_ad_drugs(),
  fill_by    = "edge_type",
  title      = "Drug Class → Drug → Gene Flow (by interaction type)",
  max_genes  = 15L
)

p_sank_typ

Key parameters:

Parameter Default Effect
fill_by "drug_class" Alluvia fill: "drug_class" or "edge_type"
min_connections 1L Minimum edges per drug–gene pair to draw
max_genes 30L Top-N genes to include

Reading the Sankey: Follow a band from left to right to trace a drug class’s exposure through individual drugs into specific biomarker genes. Bands that widen or narrow indicate unequal distribution of drug–gene connections within a class. The fill_by = "edge_type" view reveals whether a drug class predominantly inhibits, activates, or binds its targets.

Part 8: Dot matrix — Gene × Drug-Class bubble chart

plot_dgi_dotmatrix() produces a bubble/balloon plot where:

  • Y axis = genes (sorted by total connections, most-connected at top)
  • X axis = drug classes (sorted by total connections)
  • Dot size = number of drug–gene connections within that class
  • Dot colour = dominant interaction type
  • Label inside dot = exact connection count

This is arguably the most information-dense single-panel summary for a drug repurposing manuscript.

p_dot <- plot_dgi_dotmatrix(
  dgi_edges  = dgi_e,
  drug_info  = repurp_ad_drugs(),
  title      = "Gene × Drug-Class Connectivity Matrix",
  max_genes  = 15L
)

p_dot

Key parameters:

Parameter Default Effect
max_genes 40L Top-N genes by total degree
min_connections 1L Minimum connections to draw a dot
edge_palette dgi_edge_colors Interaction-type colour mapping

Reading the dot matrix: A large dot at position (gene, drug class) indicates that multiple drugs in that class target this gene. The colour reveals the mechanism: orange dots (ANTAGONIST), red (INHIBITOR), blue (AGONIST), purple (BINDER). This view is particularly useful for identifying genes that are targeted by multiple drug classes — strong candidates for multi-mechanism repurposing strategies.

Part 9: Multi-panel figures with patchwork

All ggraph/ggplot2 plot functions return a ggplot object, so they compose directly with patchwork:

library(patchwork)

# Build individual panels
p_enh <- plot_dgi_ggraph_enhanced(dgi_e, mode = "core", seed = 42L)
p_dot <- plot_dgi_dotmatrix(dgi_e, drug_info = repurp_ad_drugs())
p_ht  <- plot_dgi_heatmap(dgi_e, drug_info = repurp_ad_drugs())

# Two-column layout with shared title
(p_enh | p_dot) /
  p_ht +
  patchwork::plot_annotation(
    title    = "AD Drug–Biomarker Network Summary",
    subtitle = "Left: FR network (core). Right: dot matrix. Bottom: heatmap.",
    theme    = ggplot2::theme(plot.title = ggplot2::element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"))
  )

For igraph / circlize base-R plots in multi-panel layouts, use grDevices::recordPlot() / replayPlot() or the cowplot::draw_grob() approach:

library(cowplot)
p_ig <- cowplot::as_grob(~plot_dgi_igraph(synth_dgi))
cowplot::plot_grid(p_ig, p_enh, ncol = 2, labels = c("A", "B"))

Part 10: Comparing multiple gene sets

A common AD repurposing workflow runs the DGI pipeline separately for two differential-expression contrasts and overlays the results:

# Set 1: systemic AD vs healthy controls
dgi_sys  <- prepare_dgi_data("dgi_systemic.tsv",   gene_list = de_sys_genes)

# Set 2: non-systemic AD vs healthy controls
dgi_nsys <- prepare_dgi_data("dgi_nonsystemic.tsv", gene_list = de_nsys_genes)

# Convert both
e_sys  <- dplyr::transmute(dgi_sys,  from = Gene, to = Drug, edge_type = InteractionType)
e_nsys <- dplyr::transmute(dgi_nsys, from = Gene, to = Drug, edge_type = InteractionType)

# ggraph panels — returns ggplot, so patchwork works directly
p1 <- plot_dgi_ggraph_enhanced(e_sys,  mode = "core", title = "Systemic AD")
p2 <- plot_dgi_ggraph_enhanced(e_nsys, mode = "core", title = "Non-systemic AD")
p1 + p2

# Dot-matrix side-by-side
d1 <- plot_dgi_dotmatrix(e_sys,  drug_info = repurp_ad_drugs(), title = "Systemic AD")
d2 <- plot_dgi_dotmatrix(e_nsys, drug_info = repurp_ad_drugs(), title = "Non-systemic AD")
d1 + d2

Genes present in both sets can be highlighted by adding a shared-gene column to the node aesthetics before plotting.

Colour reference

Interaction types (dgi_edge_colors)

ec <- data.frame(
  type  = names(dgi_edge_colors),
  hex   = unname(dgi_edge_colors),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
ec$type <- factor(ec$type, levels = rev(ec$type))

ggplot2::ggplot(ec, ggplot2::aes(x = 1, y = type, fill = hex)) +
  ggplot2::geom_tile(color = "white", width = 0.9, height = 0.85) +
  ggplot2::geom_text(ggplot2::aes(label = paste0(type, "  ", hex)),
                     x = 1, size = 3.2, hjust = 0.5, color = "grey10") +
  ggplot2::scale_fill_identity() +
  ggplot2::theme_void() +
  ggplot2::labs(title = "dgi_edge_colors — interaction type palette") +
  ggplot2::theme(
    plot.title = ggplot2::element_text(size = 11, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  )

Drug classes (repurp_class_colors())

rc <- data.frame(
  class = names(repurp_class_colors()),
  hex   = unname(repurp_class_colors()),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
rc$class <- factor(rc$class, levels = rev(rc$class))

ggplot2::ggplot(rc, ggplot2::aes(x = 1, y = class, fill = hex)) +
  ggplot2::geom_tile(color = "white", width = 0.9, height = 0.85) +
  ggplot2::geom_text(ggplot2::aes(label = paste0(class, "  ", hex)),
                     x = 1, size = 3.0, hjust = 0.5, color = "grey10") +
  ggplot2::scale_fill_identity() +
  ggplot2::theme_void() +
  ggplot2::labs(title = "repurp_class_colors() — drug class palette") +
  ggplot2::theme(
    plot.title = ggplot2::element_text(size = 11, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  )

Node types (dgi_node_colors)

data.frame(
  node_type = names(dgi_node_colors),
  hex       = unname(dgi_node_colors)
)
#>   node_type     hex
#> 1      Gene #009E73
#> 2      Drug #F0E442
#> 3   Pathway #6A3D9A

Summary

All Repurp exported functions — v0.2.0.
Function Engine Output New in v0.2
prepare_dgi_data() dplyr / stringr tibble
standardize_dgi_edges() base R / dplyr tibble Yes
score_drug_repurposing() dplyr ranked tibble Yes
summarize_biomarker_coverage() dplyr summary tibble Yes
summarize_drug_classes() dplyr summary tibble Yes
score_expression_reversal() dplyr ranked tibble Yes
plot_dgi_igraph() igraph + qgraph SVG + PNG
plot_dgi_igraph_core() igraph + qgraph SVG + PNG
plot_dgi_tripartite_igraph() igraph + qgraph SVG + PNG
plot_dgi_ggraph() ggraph + tidygraph ggplot
plot_dgi_ggraph_core() ggraph + tidygraph ggplot
plot_dgi_ggraph_enhanced() ggraph + ggrepel + ggnewscale ggplot
plot_dgi_ad_network() ggplot2 + ggrepel + ggnewscale ggplot
build_visnetwork() visNetwork htmlwidget
build_visnetwork_tripartite() visNetwork htmlwidget
save_visnetwork() visNetwork HTML file path
plot_chord_dgi() circlize SVG
plot_chord_dgi_combined() circlize SVG
plot_dgi_heatmap() ggplot2 ggplot Yes
plot_dgi_sankey() ggplot2 + ggalluvial ggplot Yes
plot_dgi_dotmatrix() ggplot2 ggplot Yes
